title: Bug: Internal Error in V3TSP ("No unmarked edges found in tour") with `--threads > 1` and `-j > 1`
body:
# Internal Error in V3TSP ("No unmarked edges found in tour") with `--threads > 1` and `-j > 1`

Thanks for taking the time to review this report.

## Example / reproducer

I can reproduce this with an RTL-only `dut.v` (no C++ sources involved).
- File: [dut.v.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25743791/dut.v.gz)

## Wrong vs expected output

**Wrong (unmodified v5.046):**

```text
%Error: Internal Error: ../V3TSP.cpp:355: No unmarked edges found in tour
```

**Expected:**

Verilator should not crash with an internal error.

## Command line

From this bundle directory:

```bash
./install/verilator-bug/bin/verilator -cc \
  --error-limit 100 \
  --x-assign fast --x-initial unique \
  -Wno-WIDTHEXPAND -Wno-WIDTHTRUNC \
  -DPRINTF_COND=1 -DRANDOMIZE -DRANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT -DRANDOMIZE_REG_INIT \
  --trace-fst --assert \
  --threads 8 \
  --trace-threads 2 \
  -j 192 \
  --prefix Vdut \
  -Mdir /tmp/verilator_bug_dut.XXXXXX \
  repro/dut.v
```

## Verilator version

```text
Verilator 5.046 2026-02-28 rev v5.046
```

## OS / distro

```text
Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (Noble Numbat)
Linux 6.14.0-37-generic x86_64
```

## Did you try git master / other simulators?

- git master: yes, still fails in my environment (Verilator 5.047 devel rev v5.046-41-g44701201a)
- other simulators: not tried yet

## Root cause analysis (suspected) (by AI)

This appears to be a **data race** when Verilator runs with more than one verilation job (`-j > 1`).

When `--threads > 1`, `V3VariableOrder::orderAll()` enables the TSP-based variable ordering path (`tspSortVars()`), and it enqueues per-module sorting work into a thread pool (`V3ThreadScope::enqueue`), so multiple `V3TSP::tspSort()` calls can run concurrently in a single Verilator process.

In v5.046, `V3TSP` uses a global static counter (`V3TSP::s_edgeIdNext`) to generate `edgeId` values in `TspGraphTmpl::addEdge()`. Because this counter is not atomic and not protected by a lock, concurrent `tspSort()` calls can corrupt it (UB), leading to duplicate `edgeId` values *within a single TSP graph*. The Euler tour implementation marks edges by `edgeId`, so duplicate IDs can cause the tour to get stuck and trigger:

```text
Internal Error: ../V3TSP.cpp:355: No unmarked edges found in tour
```

## Fix (by AI)

The minimal fix is to remove the global counter and make it per-graph:

- Add `uint32_t m_edgeIdNext = 0;` to `TspGraphTmpl`
- Use `++m_edgeIdNext` instead of `++V3TSP::s_edgeIdNext`

Patch included here:
[v3tsp-edgeid-per-graph.patch.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25743886/v3tsp-edgeid-per-graph.patch.gz)

With this patch applied (still v5.046), the same command line succeeds reliably in my environment.

## May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Already fixed with `v3tsp-edgeid-per-graph.patch`
code_blocks:
%Error: Internal Error: ../V3TSP.cpp:355: No unmarked edges found in tour
./install/verilator-bug/bin/verilator -cc \
  --error-limit 100 \
  --x-assign fast --x-initial unique \
  -Wno-WIDTHEXPAND -Wno-WIDTHTRUNC \
  -DPRINTF_COND=1 -DRANDOMIZE -DRANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT -DRANDOMIZE_REG_INIT \
  --trace-fst --assert \
  --threads 8 \
  --trace-threads 2 \
  -j 192 \
  --prefix Vdut \
  -Mdir /tmp/verilator_bug_dut.XXXXXX \
  repro/dut.v
Verilator 5.046 2026-02-28 rev v5.046
Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (Noble Numbat)
Linux 6.14.0-37-generic x86_64
Internal Error: ../V3TSP.cpp:355: No unmarked edges found in tour
